body_content:
Published by TravelXploria on Mon Jun 02 2025

A city that never sleeps. It is the heart of Bollywood, the Indian film industry, and the land of dreams and opportunities, Mumbai, formerly called Bombay, the capital of Maharashtra state, is considered the industrial and financial capital of India. It is a collection of seven islands on the western coast of India facing the Arabian sea, making it rich in pristine sea beaches and ports. It is one of the highest populated cities in the world that truly reflects “unity in diversity” by having homes to the world’s richest celebrities as well as Asia’s biggest slums.


Mumbai is famous for its diverse cultural establishments, historical heritages, nightlife, street foods, and bewitching waterfronts. Thousands of tourist enthusiasts visit Mumbai from around the world every year to experience the perfect blend of art, entertainment, history, and culture of this vibrant city.


Top Tourist Attractions in Mumbai:

Mumbai offers countless tourist attractions for its visitors ranging from picturesque sea beaches to mesmerizing historical sites to gorgeous theme parks.

Here are some of the most popular attractions you might look for while booking a Mumbai tour package, but are not limited to:

• Elephanta Caves
• Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus (CST)
• Malabar Hill
• Priyadarshini Park and Sports Complex
• Gateway of India
• Haji Ali Dargah
• Siddhivinayak Temple
• Nehru Science Centre
• Juhu Beach
• Jahangir Art Gallery
• Nariman Point
• Bandra-Worli Sea Link
• Kanheri Caves
• Marine Drive
• Mahalaxmi Racecourse
• Madh Island
• Victoria Gardens
• Flora Fountain
• Film City
• Essel World


Your Mumbai Tour Package Must Include:

“The city that never sleeps”, “The city of dreams”, and “The city of seven islands” are some of a few names that describe the liveliness and restlessness of Mumbai. It offers diverse types of activities ranging from easily doable for all age groups to thrilling adventures for adventurous people. Most popular tourist activities include parasailing, cruise tour, fishing, skydiving, kayaking, banana boat riding, midnight cycling, heritage walking, as well as others.


Best Time to Visit Mumbai:

Mumbai witnesses heavy rains during the monsoons and notorious sweltering heat during the summers. Therefore, the best time to visit Mumbai is during the gap between monsoons and summers, that is during winters. Moreover, during Christmas and New Year, the city hosts some of the most exciting parties and the entire city is decorated with glaring lights, making it the peak season to visit Mumbai for outsider visitors.


How to Reach Mumbai?

Since Mumbai contributes hugely to the Indian economy and is considered the financial capital of the country, it is well connected by road, rail, and air to nearly every major city in India as well as the world. The Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus (CST) serves as the major railway station and the Chatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport serves as the primary airport covering Mumbai and Mumbai metropolitan regions.
